---
title: "Leading ML model"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
data_path <- "G:/Shared drives/Undocu Research/Data"
figures_path <- "G:/Shared drives/Undocu Research/Output/Figures"

setwd(data_path)

library(arrow)
sipp08_2 <- read_parquet("sipp_2008_w2_merged.parquet")
```

## Data Preparation

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Define the list of raw variables that need to be numeric
numeric_vars <- c(
  "TIMSTAT", "EADJUST", "EEDUCATE", "TMOVEUS", "RHCALYR", "EMS", 
  "ESEX", "ERACE", "EORIGIN", "TBRSTATE", "EHOWWELL", "TLANG1", 
  "TFIPSST", "EBORNUS", "ECITIZEN", "cit_spouse", "RMESR", 
  "THEARN", "RHPOV", "EHHNUMPP", "RCUTYP57", "RCUTYP58", 
  "ECRMTH", "RCUTYP01", "RCUTYP03", "EAFNOW", "EAFEVER", "TAGE", "TPEARN", "EHRSALL"
)

sipp08_2 <- sipp08_2 %>%
  # STEP 1: Pre-clean edge cases and convert everything to numeric
  mutate(
    # Fix the baby age string before converting to numeric
    TAGE = ifelse(TAGE == "Less than 1 full year old", "0", TAGE),
    # Convert all listed variables to numeric at once
    across(all_of(numeric_vars), as.numeric)
  ) %>%
  
  # STEP 2: The clean logic block (no more as.numeric() needed here!)
  mutate(
    # 1. IMMIGRATION & LEGAL STATUS
    undocu_entry = ifelse(TIMSTAT == 2, 1, 0), 
    undocu_likely = as.factor(ifelse(TIMSTAT == 2 & EADJUST == 2, 1, 0)),
    
    # 2. EDUCATION 
    education = case_when(
      EEDUCATE %in% 31:38 ~ "No HS diploma",          
      EEDUCATE %in% c(39, 41) ~ "HS diploma",         
      EEDUCATE == 40 ~ "Some college",                
      EEDUCATE %in% 42:43 ~ "Associate's",            
      EEDUCATE == 44 ~ "Bachelor's",                  
      EEDUCATE == 45 ~ "Master's",                    
      EEDUCATE %in% 46:47 ~ "PhD/Professional",       
      TRUE ~ "Unknown" 
    ),
    yrsed = case_when(
      EEDUCATE == 36 ~ 10,                            
      EEDUCATE == 37 ~ 11,                            
      EEDUCATE %in% c(38, 39, 40, 41) ~ 12,           
      EEDUCATE == 32 ~ 2.5,                           
      EEDUCATE == 33 ~ 5.5,                           
      EEDUCATE == 34 ~ 7.5,                           
      EEDUCATE == 35 ~ 9,                             
      EEDUCATE == 31 ~ 0,                             
      EEDUCATE %in% 42:43 ~ 14,                       
      EEDUCATE %in% c(44, 46) ~ 16,                   
      EEDUCATE == 45 ~ 17.5,                          
      EEDUCATE == 47 ~ 22,                            
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    college = ifelse(EEDUCATE %in% 44:47, 1, 0),
    hs_only = ifelse(EEDUCATE %in% 39:43, 1, 0),
    
    # 3. DEMOGRAPHICS
    # Encoding: All immig_year encoding is an approximation
    immig_yr = case_when(
      is.na(TMOVEUS) ~ 0,
      TMOVEUS == 1~1961, ## Technically
      TMOVEUS == 2~1966,
      TMOVEUS == 3~1971,
      TMOVEUS == 4~1976,
      TMOVEUS == 5~1980,
      TMOVEUS == 6~1982,
      TMOVEUS == 7~1984,
      TMOVEUS == 8~1987,
      TMOVEUS == 9~1989,
      TMOVEUS == 10~1991,
      TMOVEUS == 11~1993,
      TMOVEUS == 12~1995,
      TMOVEUS == 13~1998,
      TMOVEUS == 14~1999,
      TMOVEUS == 15~2000,
      TMOVEUS == 16~2001,
      TMOVEUS == 17~2002,
      TMOVEUS == 18~2004,
      TMOVEUS == 19~2005,
      TMOVEUS == 20~2006,
      TMOVEUS == 21~2007,
      TMOVEUS == 22~2009,
      TRUE ~ 0 # Default case
    ),
    years_us = RHCALYR - immig_yr,
    
    married = ifelse(EMS %in% c(1, 2), 1, 0),
    fem = ifelse(ESEX == 2, 1, 0),
    
    race = case_when(
      ERACE == 3 ~ "Asian",
      ERACE == 2 ~ "Black",
      ERACE == 1 ~ "White",
      ERACE == 4 ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    asian = ifelse(ERACE == 3, 1, 0),
    black = ifelse(ERACE == 2, 1, 0),
    white = ifelse(ERACE == 1, 1, 0),
    other_race = ifelse(ERACE == 4, 1, 0),
    
    spanish_hispanic_latino = ifelse(EORIGIN == 1, 1, 0),
    central_latino = ifelse(TBRSTATE == 570 & EORIGIN == 1, 1, 0), # Update 570 to your codebook value
    bpl_asia = ifelse(TBRSTATE %in% c(565:567), 1, 0), # Update to your codebook values
    
    # 4. LANGUAGE 
    english_difficult = ifelse(EHOWWELL %in% c(3, 4), 1, 0),
    nonfluent = ifelse(EHOWWELL %in% c(3, 4), 1, 0),
    english_home = ifelse(TLANG1 == -1, 1, 0),
    
    # 5. GEOGRAPHY & CITIZENSHIP
    top_ten_states = ifelse(TFIPSST %in% c(4, 6, 12, 13, 17, 34, 36, 37, 48, 53), 1, 0),
    bpl_usa = ifelse(EBORNUS == 1, 1, 0),
    citizen = ifelse(ECITIZEN == 1, 1, 0),
    cit_spouse = ifelse(cit_spouse == 1, 1, 0),
    
    # 6. EMPLOYMENT & INCOME 
    employed = ifelse(RMESR %in% c(1, 2, 3), 1, 0),
    poverty = ifelse(THEARN < RHPOV, 1, 0),
    household_size = EHHNUMPP,
    
    # --- INFLATION ADJUSTMENT (2008 to 2026 Dollars) ---)
    adj_income = TPEARN * (326.588 / 211.080),
    # Average income per month divided by average hours per week
    ## 4.34524 constant used to convert month to week
    # WAGE & HOUR CLEANING
    # Monthly Income / (Weekly Hours * 4.34524 weeks per month)
    adj_hourly = ifelse(adj_income <= 0 | EHRSALL <= 0, 
                        NA_real_, 
                        adj_income / (EHRSALL * 4.34524)),
    
    ln_adj = log(adj_hourly),
    
    # 7. BENEFITS
    medicaid = ifelse(RCUTYP57 == 1, 1, 0),
    health_ins = ifelse(RCUTYP57 == 1 | RCUTYP58 == 1, 1, 0),
    medicare = ifelse(ECRMTH == 1, 1, 0),
    social_security = ifelse(RCUTYP01 == 1 | RCUTYP03 == 1, 1, 0),
    armed_forces = ifelse(EAFNOW == 1 | EAFEVER == 1, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  filter(TAGE >= 15 & TAGE <= 55 & TPEARN >=0)


# Add this AFTER your main mutate block, before you train or predict!
sipp08_2 <- sipp08_2 %>%
  mutate(
    # Explicitly define the levels so empty categories are preserved as 0-counts
    education = factor(education, levels = c("No HS diploma", "HS diploma", 
                                             "Some college", "Associate's", 
                                             "Bachelor's", "Master's", 
                                             "PhD/Professional", "Unknown")),
    
    race = factor(race, levels = c("Asian", "Black", "White", "Other", "Unknown"))
  )

# --- POST-MUTATE LOGICAL CORRECTIONS ---
sipp08_2$bpl_foreign <- ifelse(sipp08_2$bpl_usa == 1, 0, 1)

# Because undocu_likely is a factor, we must replace with the string "0"
sipp08_2$undocu_likely <- replace(sipp08_2$undocu_likely, sipp08_2$immig_yr <= 1961, "0")
sipp08_2$undocu_likely <- replace(sipp08_2$undocu_likely, sipp08_2$armed_forces == 1 | sipp08_2$social_security == 1, "0")

sipp08_2$years_us <- ifelse(sipp08_2$years_us %in% c(2008, 2009, -1), NA, sipp08_2$years_us)
sipp08_2$age <- sipp08_2$TAGE

sipp08_2$undocu_logical <- ifelse(sipp08_2$citizen == 0 & (sipp08_2$armed_forces == 0 | sipp08_2$medicare == 0 | sipp08_2$social_security == 0), 1, 0)
sipp08_2$id <- seq_len(nrow(sipp08_2))

# 3. VARIABLE LISTS
variable_lists <- list(
  base = c("undocu_likely", "undocu_logical", "bpl_foreign", "medicaid", "central_latino", "bpl_asia", "age", "fem", "married", "cit_spouse", "nonfluent", "spanish_hispanic_latino", "household_size", "top_ten_states", "poverty", "asian", "black", "white", "other_race", "employed", "ln_adj", "years_us", "yrsed", "college"),
  
  descriptive = c("undocu_likely", "undocu_logical", "bpl_foreign", "medicaid", 
                  "central_latino", "bpl_asia", "age", "fem", "married", "cit_spouse", 
                  "nonfluent", "spanish_hispanic_latino", "household_size", "poverty", 
                  "asian", "black", "white", "other_race", "employed", "ln_adj", "years_us", "yrsed")
)

# 4. DATASET CREATOR
create_datasets <- function(data, variables) {
  datasets <- list()
  
  # Filter now works perfectly because undocu_logical is a true numeric
  undocu_filter <- data[data$undocu_logical == 1, ]
  
  # Using modern dplyr 'across()' instead of deprecated 'mutate_at'
  datasets$dTable <- as.data.frame(undocu_filter[, variables$descriptive]) %>%
    mutate(across(c(everything(), -undocu_likely), as.numeric)) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  datasets$sample <- as.data.frame(undocu_filter[, variables$base]) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  datasets$knn <- as.data.frame(undocu_filter[, variables$base]) %>%
    mutate(across(c(everything(), -undocu_likely), as.numeric)) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  # Demographic subsets now work flawlessly with == 0 or == 1
  datasets$noncit <- data[data$citizen == 0, ]
  datasets$central_latino <- data[data$central_latino == 1, ]
  datasets$spanish_hispanic_latino <- data[data$spanish_hispanic_latino == 1, ]
  datasets$top_states <- data[data$top_ten_states == 1, ]
  
  return(datasets)
}

# Execute
all_data <- create_datasets(sipp08_2, variable_lists)

all_data_college <- lapply(all_data, function(x) {
  if (is.data.frame(x) && "college" %in% names(x)) {
    x %>% filter(college == 1)
  } else {
    x  
  }
})

# Save output
# setwd("G:/Shared drives/Undocu Research/Data")
# write.csv(sipp08_2, "SIPP08_2.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

\

```{r}
set.seed(1)
library(gbm)      # For Gradient Boosting
library(class)
library(caTools)
library(ROCR)
library(caret)
library(vip)  # For feature importance
library(gridExtra)

## Data partitioning for caret library
levels(all_data$sample$undocu_likely) <- make.names(levels(all_data$sample$undocu_likely))
train_index_gbm <- createDataPartition(all_data$sample$undocu_likely, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_gbm <- all_data$sample[train_index_gbm, ]
test_gbm <- all_data$sample[-train_index_gbm, ]
```

## GBM Library (Full Training Sample)

```{r}
set.seed(1)
## Data partitioning for gbm library
train_gbm$undocu_likely <- as.numeric(train_gbm$undocu_likely) - 1      
test_gbm$undocu_likely <- as.numeric(test_gbm$undocu_likely) - 1 

gbm_model <- gbm(undocu_likely ~ age + fem + married + cit_spouse + medicaid + nonfluent + spanish_hispanic_latino + central_latino + bpl_asia + household_size + poverty + asian + black + white + other_race + employed + ln_adj + years_us + yrsed, data = train_gbm, 
                 verbose = FALSE,
                 cv.folds = 10,
                 n.trees = 765,
                 shrinkage = 0.005,
                 interaction.depth = 6)

old_gbm_model <- gbm(undocu_likely ~ age + fem + married + cit_spouse + medicaid + nonfluent + spanish_hispanic_latino + central_latino + bpl_asia + household_size + poverty + asian + black + white + other_race + employed + years_us + yrsed, data = train_gbm, 
                 verbose = FALSE,
                 cv.folds = 10,
                 n.trees = 744,
                 shrinkage = 0.005,
                 interaction.depth = 6)


## Model summary and optimal number of trees
gbm_model

optimal_trees_gbm <- gbm.perf(gbm_model, method = "cv", plot.it = FALSE)
cat("Optimal number of trees:", optimal_trees_gbm, "\n")
optimal_trees_old <- gbm.perf(old_gbm_model, method = "cv", plot.it = FALSE)
cat("Optimal number of trees:", optimal_trees_old, "\n")


setwd(figures_path)
## Feature importance
feature_importance_gbm <- vip(gbm_model, num_features = 19, bar = FALSE)
ggsave("gbm_feature_importance.png", feature_importance_gbm, width = 22, height = 16, units = "cm", dpi = 300)
grid.arrange(feature_importance_gbm, nrow = 1)

### Save data sets and model for future use ###
saveRDS(gbm_model, file = "gbm_model.rds")
saveRDS(test_gbm, file = "test_dataset.rds")
```

## GBM Library (College Training Sample)

```{r}
set.seed(1)
## Data partitioning for gbm library
all_data_college$sample$undocu_likely <- as.numeric(all_data_college$sample$undocu_likely) - 1

train_index_college <- createDataPartition(all_data_college$sample$undocu_likely, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_college <- all_data_college$sample[train_index_college, ]
test_college <- all_data_college$sample[-train_index_college, ]

college_gbm_model <- gbm(undocu_likely ~ age + fem + married + cit_spouse + medicaid + nonfluent + spanish_hispanic_latino + central_latino + bpl_asia + household_size + poverty + asian + black + white + other_race + employed + ln_adj + years_us + yrsed, data = train_college, 
                 verbose = FALSE,
                 cv.folds = 10,
                 n.trees = 549,
                 shrinkage = 0.005, 
                 interaction.depth = 6)


## Model summary and optimal number of trees
college_gbm_model

optimal_trees_college <- gbm.perf(college_gbm_model, method = "cv", plot.it = FALSE)
cat("Optimal number of trees:", optimal_trees_college, "\n")


setwd(figures_path)
## Feature importance
college_gbm_feature_importance <- vip(college_gbm_model, num_features = 19, bar = FALSE)
ggsave("college_gbm_feature_importance.png", college_gbm_feature_importance, width = 22, height = 16, units = "cm", dpi = 300)
grid.arrange(college_gbm_feature_importance, nrow = 1)

### Save model for future use ###
saveRDS(college_gbm_model, file = "college_gbm_model.rds")
```

## GBM Performance

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# GBM (FUll to Full) Library Performance
# ==============================================================================

## Metrics for Figure 2 comparison (gbm library model)
# Make predictions on test set
gbm.pred = predict(gbm_model, newdata = test_gbm, type = "response", n.trees = optimal_trees_gbm)
# Create prediction object for ROCR
gbm.prediction = prediction(gbm.pred, test_gbm$undocu_likely)
# Get raw predictions (class labels)
gbm.predict = predict(gbm_model, newdata = test_gbm, n.trees=optimal_trees_gbm)  # Class predictions

gbm.roc = performance(gbm.prediction,"tpr","fpr")   # ROC curve 
gbm.pr = performance(gbm.prediction,"prec","rec") # Precision-Recall curve
gbm.auc <- performance(gbm.prediction,"auc")@y.values[[1]] # AUC value

gbm_auc_pr <- trapz(
  as.vector(gbm.pr@x.values)[[1]][-1], 
  as.vector(gbm.pr@y.values)[[1]][-1]
)

gbm_performance <- gbm.pr
plot(gbm.roc, main = "ROC Curve", col = "blue", lwd = 2) #PLOT EACH ON A SINGLE GRAPH FOR ROC
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")
plot(gbm.pr) # plot Precision-Recall curve


cat("Optimal number of trees:", optimal_trees_gbm, "\n")
cat("AUC-ROC:", round(gbm.auc, 4), "\n")
cat("AUC-PR:", round(gbm_auc_pr, 4), "\n")

gbm_predicted_class <- ifelse(gbm.pred > 0.5, 1, 0)
table(Predicted = gbm_predicted_class, Actual = test_gbm$undocu_likely)
#plot(gbm_model) # Not same as plot for caret

# ==============================================================================
# GBM (FUll to College) Library Performance
# ==============================================================================

## Metrics for Figure 2 comparison (gbm library model)
# Make predictions on test set
gbm.pred_fc = predict(gbm_model, newdata = test_college, type = "response", n.trees = optimal_trees_gbm)
# Create prediction object for ROCR
gbm.prediction_fc = prediction(gbm.pred_fc, test_college$undocu_likely)
# Get raw predictions (class labels)
gbm.predict_fc = predict(gbm_model, newdata = test_college, n.trees=optimal_trees_gbm)  # Class predictions

gbm.roc_fc = performance(gbm.prediction_fc,"tpr","fpr")   # ROC curve 
gbm.pr_fc = performance(gbm.prediction_fc,"prec","rec") # Precision-Recall curve
gbm.auc_fc <- performance(gbm.prediction_fc,"auc")@y.values[[1]] # AUC value

gbm_auc_pr_fc <- trapz(
  as.vector(gbm.pr_fc@x.values)[[1]][-1], 
  as.vector(gbm.pr_fc@y.values)[[1]][-1]
)

gbm_performance_fc <- gbm.pr_fc
plot(gbm.roc_fc, main = "ROC Curve", col = "blue", lwd = 2) #PLOT EACH ON A SINGLE GRAPH FOR ROC
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")
plot(gbm.pr_fc) # plot Precision-Recall curve


cat("Optimal number of trees:", optimal_trees_gbm, "\n")
cat("AUC-ROC:", round(gbm.auc_fc, 4), "\n")
cat("AUC-PR:", round(gbm_auc_pr_fc, 4), "\n")

gbm_predicted_class_fc <- ifelse(gbm.pred_fc > 0.5, 1, 0)
table(Predicted = gbm_predicted_class_fc, Actual = test_college$undocu_likely)
#plot(gbm_model) # Not same as plot for caret

# ==============================================================================
# GBM (College to College) Library Performance
# ==============================================================================

## Metrics for Figure 2 comparison (gbm library model)
# Make predictions on test set
gbm.pred_cc = predict(college_gbm_model, newdata = test_college, type = "response", n.trees = optimal_trees_college)
# Create prediction object for ROCR
gbm.prediction_cc = prediction(gbm.pred_cc, test_college$undocu_likely)
# Get raw predictions (class labels)
gbm.predict_cc = predict(college_gbm_model, newdata = test_college, n.trees=optimal_trees_college)  # Class predictions

gbm.roc_cc = performance(gbm.prediction_cc,"tpr","fpr")   # ROC curve 
gbm.pr_cc = performance(gbm.prediction_cc,"prec","rec") # Precision-Recall curve
gbm.auc_cc <- performance(gbm.prediction_cc,"auc")@y.values[[1]] # AUC value

gbm_auc_pr_cc <- trapz(
  as.vector(gbm.pr_cc@x.values)[[1]][-1], 
  as.vector(gbm.pr_cc@y.values)[[1]][-1]
)

gbm_performance_cc <- gbm.pr_cc
plot(gbm.roc_cc, main = "ROC Curve", col = "blue", lwd = 2) #PLOT EACH ON A SINGLE GRAPH FOR ROC
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")
plot(gbm.pr_cc) # plot Precision-Recall curve


cat("Optimal number of trees:", optimal_trees_college, "\n")
cat("AUC-ROC:", round(gbm.auc_cc, 4), "\n")
cat("AUC-PR:", round(gbm_auc_pr_cc, 4), "\n")

gbm_predicted_class_cc <- ifelse(gbm.pred_cc > 0.5, 1, 0)
table(Predicted = gbm_predicted_class_cc, Actual = test_college$undocu_likely)
#plot(gbm_model) # Not same as plot for caret
```

## P Quartiles and Thresholds

```{r}
library(xtable)
test_gbm$p = predict(gbm_model, newdata = test_gbm, type = "response", n.trees = optimal_trees_gbm)
test_college$p = predict(gbm_model, newdata = test_college, type = "response", n.trees = optimal_trees_gbm)


# Create quartiles based on probability magnitude
test_gbm$p_quartiles <- cut(test_gbm$p, 
                                   breaks = quantile(test_gbm$p, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1)), 
                                   labels = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"), 
                                   include.lowest = TRUE)
test_college$p_quartiles <- cut(test_college$p, 
                                   breaks = quantile(test_college$p, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1)), 
                                   labels = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"), 
                                   include.lowest = TRUE)
# Check the distributions
table(test_gbm$p_quartiles)
table(test_college$p_quartiles)

# Proportion of undocu_likely in each quartiles
quartiles_table_gbm <- test_gbm %>%
  group_by(p_quartiles) %>%
  summarise(
    count = sum(undocu_likely == 1),
    n = n(),
    actual_proportion = (count / n)
    )
quartiles_table_college <- test_college %>%
  group_by(p_quartiles) %>%
  summarise(
    count = sum(undocu_likely == 1),
    n = n(),
    actual_proportion = (count / n)
    )

xtable(quartiles_table_gbm, digits=4)
print(quartiles_table_gbm)
xtable(quartiles_table_college, digits=4)
print(quartiles_table_college)


top_75_sample_num <- test_gbm %>%
  filter(undocu_likely == 1) %>%
  arrange(desc(p)) %>%
  mutate(
    rank = row_number(),
    cumulative_p = rank / n(),
    top_75 = cumulative_p <= 0.75
  ) %>%
  filter(top_75==TRUE)
threshold_75_num <- min(top_75_sample_num$p)


undocu_test_num <- test_gbm %>%
  filter(undocu_likely == 1) %>%
  arrange(desc(p))

# Create GBM high recall (p>threshold), high probability (75% of actual), low probability (Q1)
test_gbm$predicted_class <- ifelse(test_gbm$p >= threshold_75_num, 1, 0)

## Thersholds on undocu likely sample (BUG????)
quartile_thresholds_gbm <- quantile(undocu_test_num$p, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1))
print(quartile_thresholds_gbm)
```

## Precision-Recall Curves (Full to Full, Full to College, College to College)

```{r}
setwd(figures_path)
# ==============================================================================
# PREPARE PRECISION-RECALL DATA FOR ALL MODELS
# ==============================================================================

# Start with Full to Full predictions as baseline
gbm_pr_data_ff <- data.frame(
  recall = unlist(gbm.pr@x.values), 
  precision = unlist(gbm.pr@y.values)
)

# Interpolate other models to match boosted trees recall values
gbm_pr_data_fc <- data.frame(
  recall = approx(unlist(gbm.pr_fc@x.values), unlist(gbm.pr_fc@y.values), 
                 xout = gbm_pr_data_ff$recall)$x,
  precision = approx(unlist(gbm.pr_fc@x.values), unlist(gbm.pr_fc@y.values), 
                    xout = gbm_pr_data_ff$recall)$y
)

# Interpolate other models to match boosted trees recall values
gbm_pr_data_cc <- data.frame(
  recall = approx(unlist(gbm.pr_cc@x.values), unlist(gbm.pr_cc@y.values), 
                 xout = gbm_pr_data_ff$recall)$x,
  precision = approx(unlist(gbm.pr_cc@x.values), unlist(gbm.pr_cc@y.values), 
                    xout = gbm_pr_data_ff$recall)$y
)

# ==============================================================================
# COMBINE ALL DATA
# ==============================================================================

all_pr_data <- gbm_pr_data_ff

# Add precision values for other models
all_pr_data$gbm_precision_fc <- gbm_pr_data_fc$precision
all_pr_data$gbm_precision_cc <- gbm_pr_data_cc$precision

# Calculate precision differences relative to boosted trees
all_pr_data$fc_precision_difference <- gbm_pr_data_fc$precision - all_pr_data$precision
all_pr_data$cc_precision_difference <- gbm_pr_data_cc$precision - all_pr_data$precision

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 1: PRECISION-RECALL CURVES FOR ALL MODELS
# ==============================================================================

#pdf("undocumented_pr_curves.pdf", family="Times", width=9)
png("fffccc_pr_curves.png", width = 22*100, height = 16*100, res = 300, family = "Times")

op <- par(family = "serif")

plot(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$precision, type = "l", pch = 19,
     col = "yellow", xlab = "Recall", ylab = "Precision",
     lwd = 3, ylim=c(0, 1), xlim = c(0,1), lty = 1, cex.lab=1.5, cex.axis=1)

lines(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$gbm_precision_fc, type = "l", 
      col = "purple", lwd = 3, lty = 2)
lines(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$gbm_precision_cc, type = "l", 
      col = "pink", lwd = 3, lty = 3)

legend(x = 0.5, y = 1, 
       col = c("white", "yellow", "purple", "pink"),
       lwd = c(3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3), 
       lty = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5),
       legend = c("Model:", "Full to Full", "Full to College", "College to College"),
       bty = 'n', cex = 1, ncol = 1, seg.len = 6)

# Add random baseline
total_undocu <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1)
random_baseline <- total_undocu / nrow(test_gbm)
abline(h = random_baseline, lty = 2, col = "gray")
# Add text label for random baseline
text(0.5, random_baseline + 0.05, 
     paste("Random baseline:", round(random_baseline, 3)), 
     cex = 0.9, col = "gray30")

dev.off()

### Relative Pr-Re curves ###

png("undocumented_sample_differences.png", width = 22*100, height = 16*100, res = 300, family = "Times")

op <- par(family = "serif")

plot(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$fc_precision_difference, type = "l", pch = 19, 
     col = "purple", xlab = "Recall Relative to Full-to-Full mapping", ylab = "Precision", 
     lwd = 3, ylim = c(-0.3, 0.1), xlim = c(0,1), lty = 2, cex.lab=1.5, cex.axis=1)

lines(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$cc_precision_difference, type = "l", 
      col = "pink", lwd = 3, lty = 3)

legend(x = 0.5, y = -0.1, 
       col = c("white", "purple", "pink"),
       lwd = c(3, 3, 3), 
       lty = c(0, 2, 3),
       legend = c("Model:", "Full-to-College", "College-to-College"),
       bty = 'n', cex = 1, ncol = 1, seg.len = 5)

abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray")
# Add text label for random baseline
text(0.5, 0.05, 
     paste("Full-to-Full baseline:", round(0, 3)), 
     cex = 0.9, col = "gray30")
dev.off()
```

## Pr-Re Curves between Old and Wage model

```{r}
setwd(figures_path)
# ==============================================================================
# PREPARE PRECISION-RECALL DATA FOR BOTH MODELS
# ==============================================================================
gbm.pred_old = predict(gbm_model, newdata = test_gbm, type = "response", n.trees = optimal_trees_old)
gbm.prediction_old = prediction(gbm.pred_old, test_gbm$undocu_likely)
gbm.pr_variant = performance(gbm.prediction_old,"prec","rec") # Precision-Recall curve


# 1. Start with the Baseline Model predictions
# (Replace 'gbm.pr_baseline' with your actual ROCR performance object)
gbm_pr_data_base <- data.frame(
  recall = unlist(gbm.pr@x.values), 
  precision = unlist(gbm.pr@y.values)
)

# 2. Interpolate the Variant Model to match the baseline's recall values
# (Replace 'gbm.pr_variant' with your slightly modified model's object)
gbm_pr_data_variant <- data.frame(
  recall = approx(unlist(gbm.pr_variant@x.values), unlist(gbm.pr_variant@y.values), 
                 xout = gbm_pr_data_base$recall)$x,
  precision = approx(unlist(gbm.pr_variant@x.values), unlist(gbm.pr_variant@y.values), 
                    xout = gbm_pr_data_base$recall)$y
)

# ==============================================================================
# COMBINE ALL DATA
# ==============================================================================

all_pr_data <- gbm_pr_data_base
names(all_pr_data)[2] <- "precision_base" # Rename column for clarity

# Add precision values for the variant model
all_pr_data$precision_variant <- gbm_pr_data_variant$precision

# Calculate precision differences relative to the baseline model
all_pr_data$precision_difference <- all_pr_data$precision_variant - all_pr_data$precision_base

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 1: PRECISION-RECALL CURVES FOR BOTH MODELS
# ==============================================================================

png("gbm_2model_pr_curves.png", width = 22*100, height = 16*100, res = 300, family = "Times")
op <- par(family = "serif")

# Plot Baseline
plot(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$precision_base, type = "l", pch = 19,
     col = "darkblue", xlab = "Recall", ylab = "Precision",
     lwd = 3, ylim=c(0, 1), xlim = c(0,1), lty = 1, cex.lab=1.5, cex.axis=1)

# Add Variant
lines(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$precision_variant, type = "l", 
      col = "darkorange", lwd = 3, lty = 2)

legend(x = 0.5, y = 1, 
       col = c("white", "darkblue", "darkorange"),
       lwd = c(3, 3, 3), 
       lty = c(0, 1, 2),
       legend = c("Model:", "Baseline Model", "Variant Model"),
       bty = 'n', cex = 1, ncol = 1, seg.len = 6)

# Add random baseline
total_undocu <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1)
random_baseline <- total_undocu / nrow(test_gbm)
abline(h = random_baseline, lty = 2, col = "gray")

# Add text label for random baseline
text(0.5, random_baseline + 0.05, 
     paste("Random baseline:", round(random_baseline, 3)), 
     cex = 0.9, col = "gray30")

dev.off()

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 2: RELATIVE PR CURVE (DIFFERENCE)
# ==============================================================================

png("gbm_2model_differences.png", width = 22*100, height = 16*100, res = 300, family = "Times")
op <- par(family = "serif")

# Plot the difference curve
plot(all_pr_data$recall, all_pr_data$precision_difference, type = "l", pch = 19, 
     col = "darkorange", xlab = "Recall (Relative to Baseline Model)", ylab = "Precision Difference", 
     lwd = 3, ylim = c(-0.15, 0.15), xlim = c(0,1), lty = 2, cex.lab=1.5, cex.axis=1)

legend(x = 0.5, y = -0.05, 
       col = c("white", "darkorange"),
       lwd = c(3, 3), 
       lty = c(0, 2),
       legend = c("Model:", "Variant vs. Baseline"),
       bty = 'n', cex = 1, ncol = 1, seg.len = 5)

# Add 0-line representing the baseline
abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray")

# Add text label for baseline
text(0.5, 0.015, 
     paste("Baseline Level:", round(0, 3)), 
     cex = 0.9, col = "gray30")

dev.off()
```

## Demographic Precision-Recall Curve (GBM Library; Figure 3)

```{r}
# Total undocumented cases in test set
total_undocu_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1)

# Group 1: Top 10 states
recall_top_ten_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$top_ten_states == 1) / total_undocu_gbm
precis_top_ten_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$top_ten_states == 1) / 
                sum(test_gbm$top_ten_states == 1)

# Group 2: Hispanic/Latino
recall_hispanic_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$spanish_hispanic_latino == 1) / total_undocu_gbm
precis_hispanic_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$spanish_hispanic_latino == 1) / 
                   sum(test_gbm$spanish_hispanic_latino == 1)

# Group 3: Central American Latino
recall_central_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & 
                           test_gbm$central_latino ==   1) / total_undocu_gbm
precis_central_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & 
                           test_gbm$central_latino == 1) / 
                        sum(test_gbm$central_latino == 1)

# Group 4: Asian-born
recall_asia_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$bpl_asia == 1) / total_undocu_gbm
precis_asia_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$bpl_asia == 1) / 
               sum(test_gbm$bpl_asia == 1)

# Group 5: Non-fluent English speakers
recall_nonfluent_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$nonfluent == 1) / total_undocu_gbm
precis_nonfluent_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1 & test_gbm$nonfluent == 1) / 
                    sum(test_gbm$nonfluent == 1)


# Set output file
setwd(figures_path)
png("undocumented_demographic__gbm_pr_curve.png", width = 2700, height = 2100, res = 300, family = "Times")
# Or use: pdf("undocumented_demographic_pr_curve.pdf", family="Times", width=9)

# Set up plot parameters
op <- par(family = "serif")

# Main precision-recall curve
plot(gbm_pr_data_ff$recall, gbm_pr_data_ff$precision, type = "l", pch = 19,
     col = "black", xlab = "Recall", ylab = "Precision",
     lwd = 3,
     ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 1), lty = 1, 
     cex.lab = 1.5, cex.axis = 1,
     main = "")  # Add title if desired

# Add demographic group points
points(recall_top_ten_gbm, precis_top_ten_gbm, pch = 17, col = 1, cex = 1.5)
points(recall_hispanic_gbm, precis_hispanic_gbm, pch = 19, col = 1, cex = 1.5)
points(recall_central_gbm, precis_central_gbm, pch = 19, col = "gray", cex = 1.5)
points(recall_asia_gbm, precis_asia_gbm, pch = 17, col = "gray", cex = 1.5)
points(recall_nonfluent_gbm, precis_nonfluent_gbm, pch = 15, col = "darkgray", cex = 1.5)


# Demographic groups legend
legend(0.25, 1, 
       col = c("white", 1, 1, "gray", "gray", "darkgray"), 
       pch = c(19, 17, 19, 19, 17, 15),
       pt.cex = 1.5,
       legend = c("Demographic Groups:", 
                 "Top 10 states", 
                 "Hispanic/Latino",
                 "Central Latino", 
                 "Asian Born",
                 "Non-fluent English"),
       bty = 'n', 
       cex = 1.25, 
       ncol = 1, 
       bg = "white")

# Add random baseline
random_baseline_gbm <- sum(test_gbm$undocu_likely == 1) / nrow(test_gbm)
abline(h = random_baseline_gbm, lty = 2, col = "gray50")

# Add text label for random baseline
text(0.5, random_baseline_gbm + 0.05, 
     paste("Random Baseline:", round(random_baseline_gbm, 3)), 
     cex = 0.9, col = "gray30")

dev.off()



cat("\n=== DEMOGRAPHIC GROUP PERFORMANCE ===\n")
cat("Random Baseline Precision:", round(random_baseline_gbm, 4), "\n\n")

summary_df_gbm <- data.frame(
  Group = c("Top 10 states", "Hispanic/Latino", "Central Latino", 
            "Asian Born", "Non-fluent English"),
  Recall = c(recall_top_ten_gbm, recall_hispanic_gbm, recall_central_gbm, 
            recall_asia_gbm, recall_nonfluent_gbm),
  Precision = c(precis_top_ten_gbm, precis_hispanic_gbm, precis_central_gbm, 
               precis_asia_gbm, precis_nonfluent_gbm)
)

print(summary_df_gbm)

# Save as both PDF and PNG
create_demographic_plot_gbm <- function(filename_base) {
  # Function to create the plot (reusable)
  make_plot <- function() {
    par(family = "serif")
    plot(gbm_pr_data_ff$recall, gbm_pr_data_ff$precision, type = "l", pch = 19,
         col = "black", xlab = "Recall", ylab = "Precision",
         lwd = 3, ylim = c(0, 1), xlim = c(0, 1), lty = 1, 
         cex.lab = 1.5, cex.axis = 1)
    
    points(recall_top_ten, precis_top_ten, pch = 17, col = 1, cex = 1.5)
    points(recall_hispanic, precis_hispanic, pch = 19, col = 1, cex = 1.5)
    points(recall_central, precis_central, pch = 19, col = "gray", cex = 1.5)
    points(recall_asia, precis_asia, pch = 17, col = "gray", cex = 1.5)
    
    legend(0.63, 1, col = c("white", 1), lwd = 2, lty = c(0, 1),
           legend = c("Learning Method:", "Boosted Trees"),
           bty = 'n', cex = 1.25, ncol = 1)
    
    legend(0.30, 1, col = c("white", 1, 1, "gray", "gray"), 
           pch = c(19, 17, 19, 19, 17), pt.cex = 1.5,
           legend = c("Groups:", "Top 10 states", "Hispanic/Latino",
                     "Central Latino", "Asian Born"),
           bty = 'n', cex = 1.25, ncol = 1, bg = "white")
    
    abline(h = random_baseline_gbm, lty = 2, col = "gray50")
  }
  
  # Save as PDF
  pdf(paste0(filename_base, ".pdf"), family = "Times", width = 9)
  make_plot()
  dev.off()
  
  # Save as PNG
  png(paste0(filename_base, ".png"), width = 2700, height = 2100, res = 300, family = "Times")
  make_plot()
  dev.off()
}
```

# Imputing ACS

```{r Loading ACS data}
library(readr)
setwd(data_path)

ACS_variables <- c("hisp", "spanish", "race", "bpld", "empstat", "poverty", "married", "cit_spouse", "nonfluent", "bpl_asia", "asian", "black", "white", "fem", "numprec", "yrsusa1", "hinscare", "hinscaid", "undocu", "bpl_usa", "age", "yrsed", "ln_adj")  

ACS <- read_csv("ACS_foreign_noncit_age.csv")
View(ACS)
```

```{r Preparing ACS data}
library(dplyr)

ACS_sipp <- ACS %>%
  mutate(
    # Keep everything numeric! 
    spanish_hispanic_latino = ifelse(hisp == 1 | spanish == 1, 1, 0),
    central_latino = ifelse((hisp == 1) & (bpld %in% c("belize/british honduras", "costa rica", "el salvador", "guatemala", "honduras", "nicaragua", "panama", "mexico")), 1, 0),
    
    # Ensure race flags are numeric
    black = ifelse(race == "black/african america", 1, 0),
    white = ifelse(race == "white", 1, 0),
    asian = as.numeric(asian), # Assuming 'asian' is already a column, forcing numeric
    
    # Calculate other_race cleanly inside the mutate block
    other_race = ifelse(black != 1 & white != 1 & asian != 1, 1, 0),
    
    employed = ifelse(empstat == 1, 1, 0),
    poverty = ifelse(poverty < 100, 1, 0),
    
    married = as.numeric(married),
    cit_spouse = as.numeric(cit_spouse),
    nonfluent = as.numeric(nonfluent),
    bpl_asia = as.numeric(bpl_asia),
    fem = as.numeric(fem),
    household_size = as.numeric(numprec),
    
    # Add bpl_foreign (Required by your SIPP model!)
    bpl_foreign = ifelse(bpl_usa == 0, 1, 0),
    
    # Add college flag (Required by your SIPP model! Assuming you have an education variable to build this from)
    # college = ifelse(YOUR_ED_VARIABLE >= SOME_VALUE, 1, 0), 
    
    medicare = ifelse(hinscare == "yes", 1, 0),
    medicaid = ifelse(hinscaid == "has insurance through medicaid", 1, 0),
    
    # Years in US preliminary setup
    years_us = as.character(yrsusa1)
  )

# --- POST-MUTATE LOGICAL CORRECTIONS ---

# Fix years_us string traps, then force to pure numeric
ACS_sipp$years_us <- ifelse(ACS_sipp$years_us == "n/a or less than one year" & ACS_sipp$bpl_usa == 1, NA, ACS_sipp$years_us)
ACS_sipp$years_us <- ifelse(ACS_sipp$years_us == "n/a or less than one year" & ACS_sipp$bpl_usa == 0, "0", ACS_sipp$years_us)
ACS_sipp$years_us <- as.numeric(ACS_sipp$years_us)

# --- FILTERING ---
ACS_sipp_na <- ACS_sipp %>%
  filter(
    undocu == 1, 
    !is.na(years_us), 
    !is.na(medicaid), 
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(fem), 
    !is.na(married),
    !is.na(cit_spouse), 
    !is.na(nonfluent),  
    !is.na(spanish_hispanic_latino), 
    !is.na(central_latino), 
    !is.na(bpl_asia), 
    !is.na(household_size), 
    !is.na(poverty), 
    !is.na(asian), 
    !is.na(black), 
    !is.na(white), 
    !is.na(other_race), 
    !is.na(employed),  
    !is.na(yrsed)
  )

# Create your clean numeric predictor list (ensure it matches the SIPP model exactly)
ACS_cols_numeric <- c("central_latino", "bpl_asia", "medicaid", "age", "fem", "married", "cit_spouse", "nonfluent", "spanish_hispanic_latino", "household_size", "poverty", "asian", "black", "white", "other_race", "employed", "ln_adj", "years_us", "yrsed", "bpl_foreign", "college")
```

```{r}
# 1. Identify all categorical variables (factors) in the training data
factor_vars <- names(train_gbm)[sapply(train_gbm, is.factor)]

# 2. Force the ACS data to copy the exact levels and order of the SIPP data
for(col in factor_vars) {
  if(col %in% names(ACS_sipp_na)) {
    ACS_sipp_na[[col]] <- factor(ACS_sipp_na[[col]], 
                                 levels = levels(train_gbm[[col]]))
  }
}
```

## ACS Foreign Noncitizen

```{r Undocu imputation}
## Imputation method 4: GBM
setwd(data_path)

ACS_sipp_na <- ACS_sipp_na %>%
  mutate(
gbm_undocu_p = predict(gbm_model, newdata = ACS_sipp_na, n.trees = optimal_trees_gbm, type = "response"),
    
    gbm_undocu_q = case_when(
      gbm_undocu_p < quartile_thresholds_gbm[2] ~ "Q1",
      gbm_undocu_p > quartile_thresholds_gbm[2] & gbm_undocu_p < quartile_thresholds_gbm[3] ~ "Q2",
      gbm_undocu_p > quartile_thresholds_gbm[3] & gbm_undocu_p < quartile_thresholds_gbm[4] ~ "Q3",
      gbm_undocu_p > quartile_thresholds_gbm[4] ~ "Q4",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
      ),
    gbm_high_prob = ifelse(gbm_undocu_q=="Q4", 1, 0),
    gbm_low_prob = ifelse(gbm_undocu_q=="Q1", 1, 0),
    gbm_high_recall = ifelse(gbm_undocu_p > threshold_75_num, 1, 0)
  )

write.csv(ACS_sipp_na, "ACS_foreign_gbm.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
